title: Sherwin-Williams Co (SHW) CEO John Morikis on Q2 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Sherwin-Williams Co (NYSE:SHW ) Q2 2022 Earnings Conference Call July 27, 2022 11:00 AM ET Company Participants James Jaye - SVP, IR & Corporate Communications John Morikis - Chairman & CEO Allen Mistysyn - SVP, Finance & CFO Conference Call Participants Ghansham Panjabi - Robert W. Baird & Co. Christopher Parkinson - Mizuho Securities Gregory Melich - Evercore ISI Vincent Andrews - Morgan Stanley Adam Baumgarten - Zelman & Associates Stephen Byrne - Bank of America Merrill Lynch Garik Shmois - Loop Capital Markets John Roberts - Crédit Suisse Kevin McCarthy - Vertical Research Partners Adrien Tamagno - Berenberg Truman Patterson - Wolfe Research Charles Cerankosky - Northcoast Research Partners Kenneth Zener - KeyBanc Capital Markets Jeffrey Zekauskas - JPMorgan Chase & Co. David Begleiter - Deutsche Bank Eric Bosshard - Cleveland Research Company Michael Sison - Wells Fargo Securities Michael Leithead - Barclays Bank Arun Viswanathan - RBC Capital Markets Joshua Spector - UBS Operator Good morning.

title: Why Sherwin-Williams Shares Are Down Today
text: Supply chain issues and demand weakness caused the quarter to fall flat.

title: Sherwin-Williams sink after earnings miss and full-year profit warning
text: Sherwin-Williams Co. SHW, -1.82% stock sank 9.6% in Wednesday premarket trading after it posted second-quarter profit and sales that missed Street expectations and forecast full-year profit below the consensus. The paint reported net income of $577.9 million, or $2.21 per share, down from $648.6 million, or $2.42 per share last year.

title: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) to Post Q2 Earnings: What's in Store?
text: Sherwin-Williams' (SHW) Q2 performance is likely to have benefited from strong demand as well as pricing and cost-control initiatives.

title: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) Gains on Higher Demand, Expansion Moves
text: Sherwin-Williams (SHW) benefits from higher demand in architectural and industrial end markets, pricing actions and expansion of retail operations.
